name: HOUGHALL

surface_od_m.qualifier: Ref level: 38.03 m OD (log header, p2); index sheet p1 gives 'B 38.03' mOD

units: metres, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: driller's comment: 'Great quantity of water in sand and gravel and sandstone below.'
driller's comment (water) applies: 'sand and gravel and sandstone below'
mixed lithology: sandstone and mudstone; classed by first-named
driller's comment: 'Temporary lining to 21m through broken sandstone below sand and gravel.'

sum slip: 22.8 + 2.0 = 24.8 but clerk wrote depth 24.7; depth column is internally consistent with the next row (24.7 + 8.1 = 32.8), so the 0.1 m discrepancy sits in this thickness cell; verified at 4x zoom, digits read 2.0 and 24.7
construction: intermediate lining grouted to 51 metres; driller: 'Intermediate lining to 51 m. Dry after lining until 55.5 m.'
driller's term 'Sandy stone' classed as sandstone (not clearly argillaceous)
driller's comment: 'Coal wet at 60.15 m. No void.' (60.15 m is 0.05 m above this coal's logged top at 60.2 m)

notes: NRA Durham minewater observation borehole, Houghall 24-5-150, NGR NZ 2803 4038, date completed 24/09/93. Page 1 is an index/header sheet ('NRA DURHAM MINEWATER OBSERVATION BOREHOLES' listing 14 holes; Houghall row: B 38.03 mOD, 63.3 m) with no strata. Page 2 is the typed log. Handwritten filing marks 'Northern England LS, Northumbria + Yorkshire NRA', 'Houghall - 24-5-150', '27', 'NZ24/21', 'NZ24SE' — filing annotations, not seam correlations; no pencil seam marginalia and no seam names given for any of the four coals (1.2 m at 34.0, 0.3 m at 46.0, 0.2 m at 47.3, 1.3 m at 61.5). One clerk arithmetic slip at interval 9 (0.1 m, see annotation); depth column otherwise sums exactly to 63.3 m. Construction: 7" PVC casing grouted to 15 m; intermediate lining grouted to 51 metres; final lining from 48.3 to 63.3 metres. Driller's comments: great quantity of water in sand and gravel and sandstone below; temporary lining to 21 m through broken sandstone below sand and gravel; RWL before intermediate lining 11.25 m; intermediate lining to 51 m, dry after lining until 55.5 m; coal wet at 60.15 m; no void; RWL on completion 5.8 m.
